title: Sestet - Sent To A Friend With A Volume Of Tennyson
content:
Wouldst know the clash of knightly steel on steel?
    Or list the throstle singing loud and clear?
    Or walk at twilight by some haunted mere
    In Surrey; or in throbbing London feel
    Life's pulse at highest--hark, the minster's peal! . . .
    Turn but the page, that various world is here!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Thomas Bailey Aldrich, titled "Sestet - Sent To A Friend With A Volume Of Tennyson", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
